title: Datia Palace
tagline: Datia Palace — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Datia Palace, also known as Bir Singh Palace or Bir Singh Dev Palace, is situated nearly 75 km from Gwalior city in Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: Datia Palace, also known as Bir Singh Palace or Bir Singh Dev Palace, is situated nearly 75 km from Gwalior city in Madhya Pradesh. The specialty of the palace is that it has 7 floors and no member of the royal family lived in the palace. The founder of the Datia State in Bundelkhand - Maharaj Birsingh Deo Build many such 52 monuments all around the country. Datia Palace is also known as Satkhanda Palace, Datia Mahal, and Purana Mahal or the "Old Palace".

topPlaces.name: Datia
topPlaces.description: Datia is a city and the headquarters of the Datia District of Gwalior Chambal region in north Madhya Pradesh, Central India. Datia is famous for Pitambara Peeth, a shakti pilgrimage site.

topPlaces.name: Datia rail crash
topPlaces.description: The Datia train crash was a railroad crash that occurred on 3 October 2005 involving a passenger train near Datia in India's Madhya Pradesh province. The crash occurred just three weeks before the Veligonda rail disaster which killed 114 people.

topPlaces.name: Datia State
topPlaces.description: Datia State was a princely state in subsidiary alliance with British India. The state was administered as part of the Bundelkhand Agency of Central India.

topPlaces.name: Badoni
topPlaces.description: Badoni is a town and a Nagar Parishad in Datia District of Madhya Pradesh in India. A Tehsil headquarter, it is 12 km away from the district headquarters Datia.

items.note: Datia is a city and the headquarters of the Datia District of Gwalior Chambal region in north Madhya Pradesh, Central India. Datia is famous for Pitambara Peeth, a shakti pilgrimage site.

The Datia train crash was a railroad crash that occurred on 3 October 2005 involving a passenger train near Datia in India's Madhya Pradesh province. The crash occurred just three weeks before the Veligonda rail disaster which killed 114 people.
Datia State was a princely state in subsidiary alliance with British India. The state was administered as part of the Bundelkhand Agency of Central India.
Badoni is a town and a Nagar Parishad in Datia District of Madhya Pradesh in India. A Tehsil headquarter, it is 12 km away from the district headquarters Datia.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Datia Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Datia Palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Datia Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gwalior Airport (73 km) and the nearest railway station is Jhansi Junction (28 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Datia Palace?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Datia Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Datia Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Datia Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Datia Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Datia, Datia rail crash, Datia State, Badoni.
